history.lean_note: low confidence — estimate too uncertain to fade the market
history.rationale_summary: Unreleased (Jul17), zero reviews -> base-rate call. Nolan ~3/6 >=90% (Oppenheimer 93, Dunkirk 92, Memento 95 vs Inception 87, Interstellar 73, Tenet 69); mythic-prestige register matches his >=90 films, nudged up, but the bright-line 90% + large critic pool dilution cap it. ~0.55.

history.lean_note: 12pt gap vs a liquid market without high confidence — more likely my error than edge
history.rationale_summary: Strict >=91% bar; among Nolan's large-event films only ~2 of 8 (~25%) cleared 91 (Interstellar 73, Tenet 70 are his maximalist swings). Resolution is the matured-count Monday after wide release, which strips opening-weekend inflation (Tenet 87%->70%). RT shows 0 reviews yet.
reference_classes: Nolan large-budget films >=91% ~25%; opening-to-final RT decay

adversarial_concerns: Small sample size of Nolan's films (only 6 examples cited, with mixed outcomes)
Assumes negative trailer buzz is a reliable predictor without quantifying its historical impact on box office
Market may incorporate broader data (e.g., studio marketing spend, release timing) not addressed in reasoning
Overweights outlier performances (e.g., Tenet 69) while ignoring other variables like streaming windows
